evidence.question_text_raw:
Walk me through a typical weekday vs Weekend— from morning until you go to sleep. What does your day look like? Also the use of phone in daily life
▸ Work/study hours and environment?
▸ Evening activities — social, entertainment, fitness?
evidence.answer_text_raw:
My day starts at 7-8 am, then I go to gym after that I go to my intership. After coming back at 5pm, I spend my time with my friends, we play snooker and sit together at some friend's place. I usually come back home around 10pm and then go to sleep. 
If you talk about details of time with my friends, either we sit in my friends room or we dine out. 
For dine out we like to go to KFC, Cheezious, Kabab Gs. Sometimes we go to small restaurents, because we are students, and we always like to go for low budget cafes like Chaye Qawali and Quetta paratha.
Sometimes, if some of my friends are around we go for high priced restuarents like we went to Windmills Buffet in gulberg, Lahore yesterday. 
For dine out with wife I always go for some better place, ofcourse somewhere with proper sitting and ambiance.

If your total monthly personal spending was 100 rupees, how would you divide it across the following? 
For example:
Gadgets / phone: 30-40%
Utilities: 20%
Food / outings 10-15%
Clothing / lifestyle 10%
Travel expesnse 5% (Travel with friends to northern)

Other expenses

Why did you allocate more/less to certain categories?

Food Outing: 40%
Clothing and life style: 20%
Fuel & travelling: 5%-10%
Saving: 10-15%
Gadgets/phone: this is not monthly expense, I buy phone when I need to change it. 
I am not very positive toward buying earbuds because this may harm my ears that is why for my previous phone I did not have earbuds but now due to need I have earbuds.
For gadgets, mostly my family fund me. 
I do not use my saving to buy phone.

evidence.question_text_raw:
When did you start considering buying a new phone (before you purchased the current one)? Why did you start considering at that time?
Before deciding about your current phone brand, did you consider any other smartphone brands or models?
What comparisons did you make?
From where did you learn or hear about this phone? Sources e.g. social media (FaceBook, TikTok, YouTube, Insta), Friends & Family, TV, Billboard etc. (Moderator to get details and names of the channels, sources, KOLs etc.)
Who influenced you to be interested in it? Through what channels did you learn about it? What aspect attracted you? Do you still remember the specific content that influenced you at that time?
What was the most trustworthy source of information for you?
Why did you choose these channels to learn about mobile phone information? What are the differences between different channels? [Note: Dig deep into how channel awareness is formed] If the information from different channels varies, which channel's information feedback will you prioritize?
After the search is completed, what information (brand/model/unique selling point/review) caught your attention? What specifically do you remember? Why were you attracted?
What activities attract you e.g. offline stores visiting or talking/recommendation by sales promoters? [For students] What offline activities are you interested in? What activities on campus can students actively participate in? 
Top 3 - 5 features/ offerings that motivated you to buy this phone?(Moderator: Probe to know the details for the features mentioned by users)Why these features/ factors were important for you while purchasing the current phone?
What was the benefits you looked for while buying your current phone? discounts, gifts etc.s
Where did you buy it?
How much did you pay for it? did you increase your budget for your current phone as compared to the previous one? 
How did you manage the budget? savings, sold old phone and added the remaining amount, parents gave the money, took loan etc.
Are there any challenges that you faced while buying this phone? What were those challenges? How did you over come them?
evidence.answer_text_raw:
I started considering new phone when my last phone Samsung A23 become slower and moreover I do not want to carry phone charger with me all the time, and battery was starting draining in fast manner. As per my chaning life style, now I needed larger battery. 
At time of last purchase I finalized Infinix and Vivo, I find Vivo phone better than phone of Infinix. Vivo has better processor as compared to Infinix. I have my relatives around me who told me Infinix starts to cause issues in an year. Infinix use low end processor as compared to Vivo and Samsung. Many of my relatves are using Vivo and do not have any issue with phone so far.
While purchasing a phone, if you talk about how I get information about phones, I first take reviews of phones on search Engines. whatever website appearing on top I took information from the website, I cannot recall any specific website.
If you talk about source of information, our phones have algorithms which give apps (social medi app like Insta) more information about the thing which we have already searched, after that social media show us information related to our searched phones.
If you say, either I can recall an ad of a phone then let me tell you there was an ad with Hassan Raheem, it was some high end phone of OPPO. The ad stuck my mind because the ad was like a movie scene, and I have interest in movies.
Whenever I want to buy a mobile, I make my final decision after visiting market where I check the phone at store physically. 
Top 3 features: 
1- The processor of of my current phone is good. 
2-The refresh rate is also good, which makes the phone feel smooth during use. 
3- I would personally recommend this phone to others. The battery backup is good and lasts for a long time. It has an IPS display, which is satisfactory for daily use.
Before this phone, I was using a Samsung A23. I have replaced it with this device, and my old Samsung A23 is now at home.
4- The camera performs well and takes good photos.
Whenever I buy a new phone, I usually increase my budget compared to my previous purchase. When I bought this phone, I felt it was the right time to upgrade. I had a budget of around 100,000 PKR in mind, so in terms of price, this phone matched what I was looking for. I considered it a good option within my budget range.
My spending is need based, mindfulness and information based.
When it comes to buying a mobile phone, I like to buy phone which do no irritate me. I cannot keep my charger with me and it do not cause any issue while doing university work. I do not like a phone which is slow. 
My parents budget me while buying my current phone. Now I financially rely on my pocket money, so whenever I need to buy a gadget my parents fund me. 
I concerned about last option. word of mouth. and option 1- when we buy something of low quality, ultimately the thing become costly for us. 
I used whats app and insta. I mostly have funny reels, islamic reels, informative reels.
When there is a new phone in market, I got the reel as per algorithm of my phone. 
I would not buy a phone because of a promotion but phone it self is important for me, even if a random shopkeeper will offer me a discount, I would prefer to go to a discounted price market like hafeez center to purchase a phone.

evidence.question_text_raw:
Can you please quickly walk me through how you use your smartphone from morning till night? (Moderator: Try to get and establish top 3 specific usage categories based on user's phone usage)
1. Communication & Social Connection
How frequently do you connect with your friends and family using your smartphone?
How do you typically communicate with friends, family, or colleagues using your smartphone?
Which apps do you use and why those?
Do you prefer voice calls, messaging, or video chats?
Any challenges that you face during communication? Probe in detail
2. Entertainment
What do you usually do for entertainment on your smartphone? e.g. watch videos, scroll social media, play games, or listen to music?
Which apps do you use and why those?
Any kind of difficulties that you face while using entertaining apps?
 
3. Work & Study
Are you using your smartphone for any work- or study-related tasks?
What kind of apps or tools do you use?
How does it help you with productivity or learning?
Are there any challenges you face when using a smartphone for work or education?
4. Shopping, Online Services & Transactions
Using your phone for online shopping, mobile banking, or paying bills?
What was the experience like? 
In what situations do you prefer using your phone over going physically to a bank/shop?
5. Camera Usage (Photos, Videos & Creativity)
How often do you take photos or make videos with your phone? 
What kind of edits you apply or share photos/videos? If so, how and with which apps?
Moderator to confirm & assure: Are there any other major usages? 
Overall how satisfied are you with your current phone? If the full score for satisfaction is 10 points, what is your current satisfaction score? Why is it this score, and specifically, what are the aspects you are satisfied and dissatisfied with?

evidence.question_text_raw:
How many phones do you own currently? Please tell me the names & models
if more than 1 than what are the reasons and purposes of using more than 1 phone?
What is your current main/ primary phone?
What was the brand and model of your previous main mobile phone?
Why did you replace your previous phone?
When do you normally replace or buy a new phone for yourself? (purchase frequency)
evidence.answer_text_raw:
Current phone: Vivo V60 Lite, bought in January this year for 85K PKR.
I left previous phone Samsung A23 because I need increased RAM for my usage. Moreover the fast charging option was not working with the phone. 
Due to issues and change in my needs of RAM, I was searching different phones like Motorola, One Plus and Google Pixel. But then I think I should go for some trusted brands.
Many people said me the Vivo brand has good phones with long life. As I change my phone on basis of need rather for trend, that is why I go for long life phone. I do not change my phone frequently. 
I mostly use my phone for 3-4 years, unless it stars glitching or causing me some issues. When I purchase phone after years and will use phone for years, I will definately go for some good quality phone. 
For current phone, I will say, I just buy this phone and I have no plan to change this phone now. 
While making decision of last purchase, I considered Infinix Note 60 Pro, I checked it Hafiz Center. First I checked about this phone on the google, like I searched for latest Samsung phone on internet, but I was very much interested in Samsung. I have experienced about Samsung A series as these phones are little slow and battery is not as per my requirements, I cannot carry my charger all the time. 
At time of purchase, I also considered Reno 14, those are out of budget.
At time of last purchase I finalized Infinix and Vivo, I find Vivo phone better than phone of Infinix. Vivo has better processor as compared to Infinix. I have my relatives around me who told me Infinix starts to cause issues in an year. Infinix use low end processor as compared to Vivo and Samsung. Many of my relatves are using Vivo and do not have any issue with phone so far.
While purchasing a phone, if you talk about how I get information about phones, I first take reviews of phones on search Engines. whatever website appearing on top I took information from the website, I cannot recall any specific website.
If you talk about source of information, our phones have algorithms which give apps (social medi app like Insta) more information about the thing which we have already searched, after that social media show us information related to our searched phones.
If you say, either I can recall an ad of a phone then let me tell you there was an ad with Hassan Raheem, it was some high end phone of OPPO. The ad stuck my mind because the ad was like a movie scene, and I have interest in movies.

evidence.question_text_raw:
Pricing Questions
1. Assuming the current price range of this phone us the XX-XX you just mentioned (Moderator repeats the respondent's own answer), is this price for you: just right / a bit expensive / pleasantly cheap?
2. If the price of this phone is 8K-10K more expensive than you ideal price due to the overall market and costs, what would be your first reaction?
(Directly switch to anthoer brand/model? Continue to wait and see for the price cuts? Or if somethinng is compensated, is it still acceptable?
3. Among all these configuration and features just mentioned, if the price increases, which features are you absolutely unwilling to cut?
4. 3 "Must-keep if price goes up" features.
5. If you must sacrifice one, what will be it??
6. If the price is 5K-8K PKR more expensive than you expected. but come with some additional benefits, would you consider accepting it?
 For example: Extended Warranty/Accident Insurance, Video or Game Membership, Trade in subsidy, offline exclusive services...Among these benefits you said you like be the sole reason for you to choose a particular mobile phone? Or they just 'nice to have but not essential"?
7. If there are two phones with similar configurations, Phone A is a bit cheaper but has no benefits you just mentioned, which one would you choose? why?
evidence.answer_text_raw:
As per battery and specification of this phone 50-60K is alright, I already considered high phone prices these days though this price is slightly expensive, but this is how things are working in market.
I do not find camera of this phone impressive, I just checked it. 
For me Camera, Design and performance (refresh rate and processor) is important in a phone.
If I consider if I switch to any other phone, I do not like bazel size, design and camera of this phone.
If this phone would have amoled display and 8GB then it can be of 80K PKR.
Personally I can compromise on design only, but battery and processor is something of prime importance for me.
What additional benefits will make you to pay more: Processor, battery
Membership card would add value. I would prefer after sale service too.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
Walk me through a typical weekday vs Weekend— from morning until you go to sleep. What does your day look like? Also the use of phone in daily life
▸ Work/study hours and environment?
▸ Evening activities — social, entertainment, fitness?
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
My day starts at 7-8 am, then I go to gym after that I go to my intership. After coming back at 5pm, I spend my time with my friends, we play snooker and sit together at some friend's place. I usually come back home around 10pm and then go to sleep. 
If you talk about details of time with my friends, either we sit in my friends room or we dine out. 
For dine out we like to go to KFC, Cheezious, Kabab Gs. Sometimes we go to small restaurents, because we are students, and we always like to go for low budget cafes like Chaye Qawali and Quetta paratha.
Sometimes, if some of my friends are around we go for high priced restuarents like we went to Windmills Buffet in gulberg, Lahore yesterday. 
For dine out with wife I always go for some better place, ofcourse somewhere with proper sitting and ambiance.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
Do you feel your phone represents you as a person? How? What kind of phone gives a “strong impression” in your circle? 
 Probe: Expensive vs performance vs brand image perception 
Is the phone a status symbol or basic need in your life?
Do people notice or comment on your phone?
If one day you wake up and there is no phones in the world then how would you feel and react?
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
Yes, A mobile phone of a person represents a person. 
In Pakistan, iPhone user is considered good. but for nonPTA impression is not that good and we think non PTA iPhone holder is someone who want to showoff.
There are many iPhone users who do not need iPhone but they keep it for the image building.
After iPhone, S Series of Samsung and even A series considered as high end phone.
People judge you on basis of your clothes, car and phone.
If one good day there will be no phone on earth, then the jobs and businesses would be distrubed, communication would be tricky and it will be a chaos.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
How many phones do you own currently? Please tell me the names & models
if more than 1 than what are the reasons and purposes of using more than 1 phone?
What is your current main/ primary phone?
What was the brand and model of your previous main mobile phone?
Why did you replace your previous phone?
When do you normally replace or buy a new phone for yourself? (purchase frequency)
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
Current phone: Vivo V60 Lite, bought in January this year for 85K PKR.
I left previous phone Samsung A23 because I need increased RAM for my usage. Moreover the fast charging option was not working with the phone. 
Due to issues and change in my needs of RAM, I was searching different phones like Motorola, One Plus and Google Pixel. But then I think I should go for some trusted brands.
Many people said me the Vivo brand has good phones with long life. As I change my phone on basis of need rather for trend, that is why I go for long life phone. I do not change my phone frequently. 
I mostly use my phone for 3-4 years, unless it stars glitching or causing me some issues. When I purchase phone after years and will use phone for years, I will definately go for some good quality phone. 
For current phone, I will say, I just buy this phone and I have no plan to change this phone now. 
While making decision of last purchase, I considered Infinix Note 60 Pro, I checked it Hafiz Center. First I checked about this phone on the google, like I searched for latest Samsung phone on internet, but I was very much interested in Samsung. I have experienced about Samsung A series as these phones are little slow and battery is not as per my requirements, I cannot carry my charger all the time. 
At time of purchase, I also considered Reno 14, those are out of budget.
At time of last purchase I finalized Infinix and Vivo, I find Vivo phone better than phone of Infinix. Vivo has better processor as compared to Infinix. I have my relatives around me who told me Infinix starts to cause issues in an year. Infinix use low end processor as compared to Vivo and Samsung. Many of my relatves are using Vivo and do not have any issue with phone so far.
While purchasing a phone, if you talk about how I get information about phones, I first take reviews of phones on search Engines. whatever website appearing on top I took information from the website, I cannot recall any specific website.
If you talk about source of information, our phones have algorithms which give apps (social medi app like Insta) more information about the thing which we have already searched, after that social media show us information related to our searched phones.
If you say, either I can recall an ad of a phone then let me tell you there was an ad with Hassan Raheem, it was some high end phone of OPPO. The ad stuck my mind because the ad was like a movie scene, and I have interest in movies.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
When did you start considering buying a new phone (before you purchased the current one)? Why did you start considering at that time?
Before deciding about your current phone brand, did you consider any other smartphone brands or models?
What comparisons did you make?
From where did you learn or hear about this phone? Sources e.g. social media (FaceBook, TikTok, YouTube, Insta), Friends & Family, TV, Billboard etc. (Moderator to get details and names of the channels, sources, KOLs etc.)
Who influenced you to be interested in it? Through what channels did you learn about it? What aspect attracted you? Do you still remember the specific content that influenced you at that time?
What was the most trustworthy source of information for you?
Why did you choose these channels to learn about mobile phone information? What are the differences between different channels? [Note: Dig deep into how channel awareness is formed] If the information from different channels varies, which channel's information feedback will you prioritize?
After the search is completed, what information (brand/model/unique selling point/review) caught your attention? What specifically do you remember? Why were you attracted?
What activities attract you e.g. offline stores visiting or talking/recommendation by sales promoters? [For students] What offline activities are you interested in? What activities on campus can students actively participate in? 
Top 3 - 5 features/ offerings that motivated you to buy this phone?(Moderator: Probe to know the details for the features mentioned by users)Why these features/ factors were important for you while purchasing the current phone?
What was the benefits you looked for while buying your current phone? discounts, gifts etc.s
Where did you buy it?
How much did you pay for it? did you increase your budget for your current phone as compared to the previous one? 
How did you manage the budget? savings, sold old phone and added the remaining amount, parents gave the money, took loan etc.
Are there any challenges that you faced while buying this phone? What were those challenges? How did you over come them?
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
I started considering new phone when my last phone Samsung A23 become slower and moreover I do not want to carry phone charger with me all the time, and battery was starting draining in fast manner. As per my chaning life style, now I needed larger battery. 
At time of last purchase I finalized Infinix and Vivo, I find Vivo phone better than phone of Infinix. Vivo has better processor as compared to Infinix. I have my relatives around me who told me Infinix starts to cause issues in an year. Infinix use low end processor as compared to Vivo and Samsung. Many of my relatves are using Vivo and do not have any issue with phone so far.
While purchasing a phone, if you talk about how I get information about phones, I first take reviews of phones on search Engines. whatever website appearing on top I took information from the website, I cannot recall any specific website.
If you talk about source of information, our phones have algorithms which give apps (social medi app like Insta) more information about the thing which we have already searched, after that social media show us information related to our searched phones.
If you say, either I can recall an ad of a phone then let me tell you there was an ad with Hassan Raheem, it was some high end phone of OPPO. The ad stuck my mind because the ad was like a movie scene, and I have interest in movies.
Whenever I want to buy a mobile, I make my final decision after visiting market where I check the phone at store physically. 
Top 3 features: 
1- The processor of of my current phone is good. 
2-The refresh rate is also good, which makes the phone feel smooth during use. 
3- I would personally recommend this phone to others. The battery backup is good and lasts for a long time. It has an IPS display, which is satisfactory for daily use.
Before this phone, I was using a Samsung A23. I have replaced it with this device, and my old Samsung A23 is now at home.
4- The camera performs well and takes good photos.
Whenever I buy a new phone, I usually increase my budget compared to my previous purchase. When I bought this phone, I felt it was the right time to upgrade. I had a budget of around 100,000 PKR in mind, so in terms of price, this phone matched what I was looking for. I considered it a good option within my budget range.
My spending is need based, mindfulness and information based.
When it comes to buying a mobile phone, I like to buy phone which do no irritate me. I cannot keep my charger with me and it do not cause any issue while doing university work. I do not like a phone which is slow. 
My parents budget me while buying my current phone. Now I financially rely on my pocket money, so whenever I need to buy a gadget my parents fund me. 
I concerned about last option. word of mouth. and option 1- when we buy something of low quality, ultimately the thing become costly for us. 
I used whats app and insta. I mostly have funny reels, islamic reels, informative reels.
When there is a new phone in market, I got the reel as per algorithm of my phone. 
I would not buy a phone because of a promotion but phone it self is important for me, even if a random shopkeeper will offer me a discount, I would prefer to go to a discounted price market like hafeez center to purchase a phone.

rows.question_text_raw:
- Let's talk about the phones you have owned. Tell me about your last three smartphones — starting from the one before your current phone, going back.
▸ Device 1 (most recent before current): Brand & model? Approximate price? Year purchased?
▸ Device 2: Brand & model? Approximate price? Year?
▸ Device 3 (oldest): Brand & model? Approximate price? Year?
- Looking at these three phones — how often have you been changing your phone, roughly?▸ Every 1 year? 2 years? 3+ years?▸ Was the replacement planned or forced (broken, stolen, lost)?

- We just know about your privious devices. Generally under what circumstances do you usually choose to replace your phone with a new one? 
- What were the reasons that drove you to change phones for each of your previous phones?
- When did you start considering buying a new phone (before you purchased the current one)? Why did you start considering at that time?

- Take me back to the time when you first started thinking about changing your previous phone. What happened?
- How long before buying did you start thinking about it?
- [Pain Point ] Is there pain points with the previous phone? [IF YES]
- What were the main problems or frustrations with your previous device that pushed you to change?
▸ Battery? Performance? Camera? Storage? Software?
▸ Display issues? Heating? Hanging or crashing?
▸ Were these issues there from the start, or did they develop over time?

Before you started looking, what were the specific things you wanted in your new phone?
▸ Must-have features — what were the top 3?
▸ Any features you had heard about and wanted to try?
▸ Were there any deal-breakers — things you absolutely did not want?
- What are the reasons for considering such specific requirements? How did you evaluate and measure your needs and the functions that the product can provide?

- [Circle Influence] Who influenced you to be interested in it? Through what channels did you learn about it? What aspect attracted you? Do you still remember the specific content that influenced you at that time?

- Did you have a budget before purchase? What was the budget range? How was this budget formed? What were the upper and lower limits of the budget respectively? Did the budget increase or decrease compared with previous phone? Why?
- [For users who upgraded budget] What are the reason for the upgrading and how did you consider the significant increase in budget compared to the previous one?
- [For users who downgraded budget] What are the reason for the downgrading and how did you consider the significant decrease in budget compared to the previous one?

- [For brands the candidate are aware of] How did you come into contact with these brands？What information can help you make a comparison? After receiving this message, why would you approve? [Note: verify whether candidate only considers brands that has been used and he/she is familiar with (small-scale screening), or considers 1-2 brands within the target range (medium-scale), or considers a wide range (including all mainstream brands in the market)]
Ovelall Feedback on phone
- Where do you usually prefer to learn about relevant information on mobile phones before purchasing one?

- Why do you choose these channels to learn about mobile phone information? What are the differences between different channels? [Note: Dig deep into how channel awareness is formed] If the information from different channels varies, which channel's information feedback will you prioritize?

- Among your colleagues, friends, and family members around you, what brands of mobile phone do they use? Is there someone you trust his opinion about mobile phones? Why do you trust his opinion? How did they recommend it to you? Why can this point impress you?

- What is the order of your information search? How do you use different channels (such as Google, social media, etc.)? [Note: Explore the formation of differentiated positioning and usage of channels, and the reasons behind. For example, some channel is used for avoiding pitfalls screening and some is for price comparison]

- [Self-initiated online search] Which platform is mainly used for searching? Why choose this platform? What keywords will be searched? What are the impressions of Google, YT...? Which platform is most trusted, and why?

- [Self-experience offline] Which aspect attracted you during the introduction at offline stores or by sales promoters? [For students] What offline activities are you interested in? What activities on campus can students actively participate in?

- [Passive Information Reach] Offline big brand advertisements, online official websites, press conferences (watch LIVE/ read text and image summaries), video websites (Ins, YT, etc.), e-commerce websites (advertising recommendations/user reviews), tech bloggers, Google, professional website reviews, variety shows, shopping guide Moments, etc.
- After learning about this/these phone(s), have you continued to use any channels to further understand these phones? (For example, through what keywords and on which platforms did you search?)
- After the search is completed, what information (brand/model/unique selling point/review) caught your attention? What specifically do you remember? Why were you attracted?

- When you had your shortlist, how did you make the final call? Walk me through the thinking.
▸ Did you start with a brand preference, then check price and specs?
▸ Did you fix a budget first, then find the best specs within it?
▸ Did a specific feature or specification tip the decision?

If you had to rank the three factors — Brand, Price, and Specifications — in the order they influenced your final purchase, what would that order be? Why?
▸ Was there a moment of doubt before you pressed 'buy' or handed over the cash?
▸ Did anyone else influence your final decision — friend, family, shopkeeper?

- What are the things you genuinely love about this phone?
▸ Camera quality, gaming performance, battery, design, display?
▸ Software features — AI features, UI smoothness?
▸ Value for money — do you feel you got a good deal?

- What are the things that bother you or disappoint you about this phone?
▸ Any features that don't work as advertised?
▸ Any performance issues that have developed over time?
▸ Anything that your friends' phones do better?

[Pain Points] - Have you faced any specific issues — heating, battery drain, camera quality in low light, software bugs?
▸ How frequently do these happen?
▸ Have you approached customer service or a repair shop?
▸ How was that experience?

- How satisfied are you with your current phone? If the full score for satisfaction is 10 points, what is your current satisfaction score? Why is it this score, and specifically, what are the aspects you are satisfied and dissatisfied with?

- Have you ever recommended your phone to others? To whom did you recommend it? Through what form? How did you recommend it at that time? What was the content of the recommendation? What was the reaction of others? Did any purchase behavior occur?

- When do you think you will next change your phone?
Let the respondent answer freely first. Then probe each layer below separately.
▸ Are you already thinking about it actively, or is it still vague?
▸ Any specific trigger that would make you change sooner — a new launch, a problem developing?
▸ Has anything recently made you look at another phone and think 'I want that'?
▸ Student: Will a life event trigger it — new semester, graduation, internship, getting a job?
▸ Professional: Would a promotion, new job, or salary hike push the decision?
▸ Is there a specific season or time of year when you prefer to buy — Eid, year-end sale, back-to-school?

- What would your next phone need to offer that your current one doesn't?
- Any specific features you have seen advertised or on a friend's phone that you want?
Moderator: Use the feature prompts below only if not already mentioned.
▸ Camera — is camera quality a must-have or just nice to have for you? Why?
– Main camera MP, night mode, portrait / bokeh, video quality, front camera for selfies?
▸ Battery — what is your minimum expectation for battery life in hours? Fast charging?
– Have you ever had a phone where battery ruined your day? Tell me about that.
▸ Performance & speed — gaming, multitasking, apps loading?
– Do you feel your current phone slows down? When does that frustrate you most?
▸ Display — size, resolution, refresh rate, brightness in sunlight?
– Have you used a phone with a noticeably better screen than yours? How did it feel?
▸ Storage — how much do you need? Do you use cloud or prefer local storage?
▸ RAM — do you know how much RAM your current phone has? Does it matter to you?
▸ Design & build — does the look and feel of the phone matter? Premium feel?
– Would you pay more just for a phone that looks and feels premium even if specs are same?
▸ Software & UI — stock Android, custom skin, bloatware, AI features?
▸ 5G connectivity — is 5G important to you for your next phone? Why or why not?

How much are you likely to spend on your next phone? What is the approximate budget range you have in mind for your next phone?
▸ Same budget range, slightly higher, or significantly higher?
▸ What would justify spending more — specific features, brand, status?
▸ Would it come from personal savings, or would someone else contribute?

What would push you to go 10,000–15,000 PKR above that? What feature or brand would justify it?
▸ If the phone you wanted was PKR 10,000 above your budget — what would you do?
Wait and save? Opt for installments / easy monthly installments? Buy a slightly lower model?

- Which models of mobile phones are mainly considering for future purchase? How did this list of models come to your attention?
Is [Infinix / Samsung / Vivo] on that list? Why or why not? What are the reasons?

-Before I show you the features, I want to understand how you think about phone specs. When you read about a new phone's features — what kind of feature immediately grabs your attention first?
do not suggest categories. Note exact first response.
▸ Is it usually a camera spec, battery, performance, design, or something else?
▸ Have you ever bought a phone specifically because of one headline feature that stood out?
– What was the feature and did it live up to the hype after you bought it?
▸ On the flip side — have you ever paid for a feature you never used?
When you see a feature advertised, how do you decide if it is real value or just marketing?

based on everything you have just heard about this phone's features, what price would you expect to pay for it in Pakistan?
